title: Reusable Client Architecture, Transport Abstractions, Middleware, and Configuration · Automation and Data Client Systems · Program Defense
summary: A production data release board evaluating a changed source contract must defend independent decisions across fresh cases, including one ambiguous failure and one changed boundary for reusable client architecture, transport abstractions, middleware, and configuration, producing small composable client library boundary. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a Python 3.14 urllib and HTTP client layers small composable client library boundary. Learners progress throug…
objectives: Centralize base authority, transport, timeout, user agent, credentials, limits, and defaults in an explicit client configuration. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Inject the narrow request execution capability needed for deterministic tests without cloning the whole HTTP library. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.

Compose authentication, retries, metrics, tracing, caching, and redaction with documented ordering and ownership. Demonstrate the result through Python 3.14 urllib, http.client, ssl, typed protocols, and fixture transports.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""http-clients-python cumulative safe data-client portfolio.

Browser labs use deterministic request and response models only. Live urllib,
DNS, TLS, sockets, concurrency, and load require local Python 3.14 transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RequestEvidence:
    method: str
    target: str
    attempt: int

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class ResponseEvidence:
    status: int
    body_bytes: int
    valid: bool

print("Python HTTP client portfolio ready")

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using validate and environment plus specific case evidence.
